steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:3002/, select Ethereum (1) chain, confirm USDC→WETH defaults, amount 1000
steps.expected: Form loads with Ethereum chain, no wallet connected
steps.observed: Form loads with Ethereum (1), Connect Wallet button visible (no wallet connected), USDC (0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48) → WETH (0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2), amount 1000

steps.action: Click Compare Quotes to fetch results, then click Curve tab
steps.expected: Curve result card with Swap button visible, no Approve button (no sender means no approval data)
steps.observed: Curve tab shows ALTERNATIVE result: 0.503380039303158938 WETH, Via Curve, Gas Price 0.2456 gwei. Only a Swap button present — no Approve button, consistent with contract note that Curve approval requires sender address

steps.action: Disable auto-refresh timer to prevent interference, inject mock ERC-6963 wallet (Mock Cross005 Wallet) with request logging
steps.expected: Mock wallet available for selection, request log tracking active
steps.observed: Auto-refresh intervals cleared. Mock ERC-6963 wallet injected with uuid cross005-r2-mock-wallet, logging all provider.request calls to window.__mockWalletLog

steps.action: Click Swap button on Curve card with no wallet connected
steps.expected: Wallet provider menu appears (auto-connect flow triggered)
steps.observed: Wallet provider menu appeared showing two options: Frame and Mock Cross005 Wallet. Swap button became inactive while awaiting wallet selection.

steps.action: Select Mock Cross005 Wallet from provider menu
steps.expected: Wallet connects (eth_requestAccounts), then swap auto-submits (eth_sendTransaction) without requiring a second Swap click
steps.observed: Wallet connected immediately. Mock Cross005 Wallet shown with address 0x1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678. Curve card displays 'SUCCESS: Success' status. No second Swap click was needed — the swap auto-submitted after connect.

steps.action: Read mock wallet request log to verify call order
steps.observed: Full request sequence: (1) eth_requestAccounts → (2) eth_call to USDC contract (balance check) → (3) eth_call to WETH contract (balance check) → (4) eth_chainId → (5) eth_sendTransaction to Curve router 0x45312ea0eff7e09c83cbe249fa1d7598c4c8cd4e with from=0x1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678 → (6) eth_getTransactionReceipt (polling for confirmation). eth_requestAccounts at T+0ms, eth_sendTransaction at T+3ms — confirms auto-swap with no user re-click.

screenshots: cross-005-r2-01-initial-no-wallet.png (Ethereum chain, no wallet connected, USDC→WETH form)
cross-005-r2-02-curve-results-no-wallet.png (Curve tab with Swap button, no wallet, no Approve button)
cross-005-r2-03-swap-wallet-menu.png (Wallet provider menu appeared after clicking Swap — Frame and Mock Cross005 Wallet options)
cross-005-r2-04-auto-swap-after-connect.png (Wallet connected, Curve result showing balances, auto-swap completed)
evidence.consoleErrors: Only favicon.ico 404 (cosmetic, known issue — ignored per mission guidance)
evidence.network: Mock wallet request log confirms auto-connect + auto-swap flow

frictions.description: Auto-refresh timer (15s interval) re-fetched comparison results after wallet connected, replacing the Curve card that showed SUCCESS status. Required clearing all intervals before testing to capture the swap result cleanly.
frictions.resolution: Cleared all setInterval timers via brute-force clearInterval loop before clicking Swap

summary: Tested 1 assertion (VAL-CROSS-005): pass. With no wallet connected and Curve results visible, clicking Swap triggered the wallet provider menu. After selecting Mock Cross005 Wallet, the wallet connected (eth_requestAccounts) and the swap auto-submitted (eth_sendTransaction to Curve router 0x45312ea0eff7e09c83cbe249fa1d7598c4c8cd4e) without requiring a second Swap click. The mock wallet request log confirms the exact sequence: eth_requestAccounts → eth_sendTransaction. The prior round's failure was a misread — the contract specifically tests the Swap auto-connect flow (not Approve), and notes that Curve Approve is unavailable when disconnected due to @curvefi/api requiring a sender address for approval routing.
